macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw equities slip as Treasury yields climbed further — the 10-year note hit 4.67% and the 30-year touched 5.14% amid accelerating inflation fears. Oil swung on fresh US-Iran combat fears, with the US sanctioning 19 vessels and Trump postponing a planned strike, while WTI settled at $103.81. NVDA reports tomorrow in a market grappling with hawkish Fed expectations and ongoing Hormuz disruption.
commodities_note: WTI held above $103 as the Strait of Hormuz crisis enters its 30th day, with new US sanctions on 19 vessels escalating the crackdown on Iranian oil. Gold edged up 0.25% to $4,474.5, while the DXY rose 0.05% to 99.39. The US10Y's climb to 4.67% continues to pressure risk assets broadly.

singapore.summary: All three banks are hard-blocked on technical extension and NIM compression from elevated US10Y (4.67%) and MAS S$NEER policy. DBS is the most overbought (RSI 76) while UOB shows slightly better RSI (66). No relief until US10Y breaks below 4.20% or MAS shifts, likely October 2026.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — awaiting US10Y reversal and MAS dovish pivot.
singapore.key_development: US10Y rose to 4.67% today, moving further away from the 4.20% NIM-relief threshold.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster remains universally CAUTION, with 13 of 22 watchlist names hard-blocked. NVDA earnings tomorrow is the pivotal event — a strong beat could re-rate the group, but broad extension and bond yield headwinds keep entries on hold. SOXX -0.06% today; outperformance vs. BigTech is negligible.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — awaiting NVDA earnings catalyst.
semiconductors.key_development: NVDA reports tomorrow; ASML High-NA EUV milestone adds long-term conviction.

big_tech.summary: All four BigTech names are in CAUTION — AMZN and GOOG hard-blocked, MSFT near its 50-day but with OpenAI revenue opacity, PLTR extended on valuation. The group is under pressure from rising yields and inflation fears, with the IRS 10Y above 4.6% making duration-sensitive mega-caps unattractive.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but rate headwinds dominate near-term.
big_tech.key_development: OpenAI Guaranteed Capacity offering could boost Azure demand, but doesn't resolve credit circularity.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B investment from NVDA for AI photonics alongside LITE — direct beneficiary of NVDA optical interconnect buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's next earnings confirm AI optics revenue acceleration above $800M quarterly run-rate.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: Snapdragon AI edge chips leverage Arm architecture — on-device AI inference play that parallels NVDA data-center buildout, benefiting from same AI capex cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor if NVDA earnings signal enterprise AI shift to edge computing; QCOM's AI PC chip wins would be the catalyst.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Leading supplier of high-speed data center switches; directly tied to hyperscaler capex (MSFT, AMZN, GOOG) and NVDA Spectrum-X networking.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after NVDA earnings; if Blackwell Ultra ramp drives 800G switch demand, ANET becomes actionable on a 10% pullback.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz oil blockade: WTI $103.81, US sanctions 19 vessels, Strait of Hormuz effectively closed for 30th day, military escalation risk persists.
US inflation and Fed hawkish pivot: CPI 3.8% YoY, PPI 6%, bond yields surging (US10Y 4.67%, 30Y 5.14%) with market pricing rate hike by year-end.
US-China trade friction: Trump-Xi summit yields narrow harvest; chip export controls remain a dispute, no new tech restrictions but supply chain uncertainty.
Tariff uncertainty from Section 122 and IEEPA ruling: Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs, admin pivoted to Section 122, but trade court ruling keeps 10% baseline for now.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil and bond yields are compressing tech valuations — Semis and BigTech clusters are universally CAUTION. Singapore banks are doubly squeezed by higher USD funding costs and MAS S$NEER strength. The NVDA earnings binary tomorrow could shift risk appetite if guidance surprises.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US Treasury sanctioned 19 vessels and entities in expanded crackdown on Iranian oil exports (per World Oil, May 20).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold fire on new entries until after NVDA earnings and any sign of easing in bond yields — cash is a position in this environment.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 13 of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; NVDA earnings tomorrow and persistent inflation pose near-term binary risk.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt.
